pipeline_health.open_issues_note: Up from 4 to 32 — reflects massive churn-and-burn work creating issues faster than they close. Active pipeline, not a stall. Notable: ISS-867 (n(r) defense preemption), ISS-837 (stale counts in SEC-1.7), ISS-835 (B(r) formula error).

findings.description: ALL THREE OPUS AGENTS AT ACCELERATED SCHEDULES WITH STALE BAU METADATA: dome-analyst (*/30), dome-decider (*/30), dome-curmudgeon (hourly) are all running at churn-and-burn speeds. But priority-queue.json mode='bau' since 2026-04-11T10:42Z and all schedule_state intervals show BAU values (analyst=120min, curmudgeon=240min, decider=240min). The decider's schedule_check field in recent reports is empty {} — Step E4 isn't even logging. The acceleration appears justified (15 queue items, 32 open issues, 45 pending expansions) but the metadata is completely disconnected from reality.
findings.evidence: list_scheduled_tasks: analyst=*/30, decider=*/30, curmudgeon=0 * * * *. priority-queue.json schedule_state: analyst=120min, curmudgeon=240min, decider=240min, mode=bau. Decider reports daily-report-2026-04-13T06-14.json through T14-30: schedule_check={} (empty). Task descriptions explicitly say 'churn-and-burn'.
findings.suggested_fix: Two fixes needed: (1) Update priority-queue.json schedule_state to reflect actual crons (analyst=30, curmudgeon=60, decider=30, mode=churn-and-burn). (2) Fix Step E4 in decider.md to call list_scheduled_tasks and compare actual crons against schedule_state metadata. When mismatch: either update metadata to match reality, or flag for human review.

findings.description: 2 ORPHAN PROPOSALS NOT IN LEDGER: proposal-ISS-800-resolution.json and proposal-ISS-837-resolution.json exist on FUSE but are not in processed-proposals.json ledger. These will be re-processed by the decider on every run, wasting tokens on already-processed content.
findings.evidence: 30 proposal files on disk, 28 in ledger. Missing: proposal-ISS-800-resolution.json, proposal-ISS-837-resolution.json.
findings.suggested_fix: Decider should add these to the ledger after processing. If already processed, manually add filenames to monitor/analyst/processed-proposals.json files array.

recommendations.description: FIX METADATA-VS-REALITY: Update priority-queue.json schedule_state to reflect actual churn-and-burn state (analyst=30min, curmudgeon=60min, decider=30min, mode=churn-and-burn). This is immediate data correction.

FIX STEP E4 DESIGN FLAW (escalated from yesterday — now affecting all 3 agents): decider.md Step E4 must call list_scheduled_tasks and compare actual cron expressions against schedule_state metadata. When mismatch detected: either update metadata to match (if intentional acceleration) or log schedule_action_needed (if drift). This is the root cause of all metadata-reality discrepancies.

tinker_self_audit.self_assessment: Substantive run. Key new finding: all three Opus agents are back in churn-and-burn but metadata was never updated — the same Step E4 bug from yesterday, now worse. Also found 2 orphan proposals causing re-processing. CLAUDE.md bloat from yesterday resolved (down 34%). Pipeline is in healthy high-throughput mode; cost focus shifts to metadata accuracy and BAU-transition readiness.

notes_to_future_tinker: The metadata-vs-reality gap is now the #1 systemic issue. When the queue drains and BAU restore triggers, the Step E4 bug will again produce a silent mismatch. Priority: get Step E4 fix implemented before the next BAU transition. PROP-005 is at 7 reports — at this point consider filing a new combined PROP that bundles Step E4 fix + schedule descriptions + PROP-005 as a single 'decider hygiene' patch to reduce implementation friction. The 2 orphan proposals (ISS-800, ISS-837) should be ledger-fixed next Mode 4 run. Reference file growth (11%) is moderate — mostly from decider reference expansion — watch for continued creep.
